segments.text: All right, let's jump in.
Hi, everybody.
My name is Vanessa Sigurdson.
I'm a strategic partnerships manager with
Autodesk responsible for the Fusion Technology Partner Program.
I build technology partner ecosystems in design and
manufacturing that help to expand product capability,
digitize workflows, and unlock new ways of working.
I'm going to talk to you today about what is
changing in manufacturing workflows and what's actually
unlocking that change.
It's not technology, but how things come together to create
better outcomes for our customers.
So here's the reality.
Manufacturers aren't really struggling with tools.
They're struggling with siloed workflows.
Across quoting, design, engineering,
manufacturing, and the supply chain, work is
constantly being handed off between systems.
And every time that happens, things slow down.
Data gets reentered, errors creep in, decisions get made
without the full picture.
So if you're coming from architecture, engineering, and
construction, this probably feels pretty familiar.
It's different tools, different teams, but really the same issue.
So the problem isn't capability.
It's the flow from one solution to the next.
And that shows up in real ways, longer lead times, more
rework, and missed opportunities.
At the same time, a lot is changing.
Today, we're at a real inflection point.
AI is getting better at supporting decisions.
Automation is taking out repetitive work.
And data is finally becoming more accessible across systems.
And that isn't theoretical.
Nearly 70% of our customers today are already using AI.
On their own, each of these developments
brings incremental improvement.
But when they come together, that's
where things really start to change.
Because no single solution solves the full end-to-end
workflow.
So that's where we're seeing a big shift,
from using tools to connecting them.
And even adding one connection into a workflow
can make a real big difference.
Less manual work, better accuracy, faster execution,
and then, as you build from there, that value starts to scale.
So the next question is, where do you connect?
Autodesk Fusion is where things come together.
Fusion is Autodesk's solution for a connected design
and manufacturing workflow.
It brings together computer-aided design, engineering,
electronics, and manufacturing in one modern environment.
That alone removes a lot of friction for our customers.
And it's a big reason why Fusion has
grown so quickly over the last decade.
Now, here's the important point.
Fusion isn't just where the work happens.
It's also where workflows can be extended.
When you connect your solution to Fusion,
you're not plugging into an empty system.
You're plugging into context.
Design data, manufacturing intent, real workflows
that are already in motion.
And that context is what makes integrations more useful.
Even one connection can remove a bottleneck
or improve a key decision.
And more of these connections build on each other.
At that point, that's where you start
to see compounding value emerge.
So what does this look like?
Think about a simple workflow from quoting
to design and engineering to manufacturing
and then the supply chain.
Today, these are barely connected.
And as a result, work stops and starts at each boundary.
And data doesn't flow cleanly.
But when you connect them, you remove those walls.
And instead of steps, you get flow.
And that lines up with what we're seeing.
Today, most of our customers are already
using two to three tools within a single workflow.
That's where customers really start to see the difference.
So let's make this real.
Let me show you how some of our partners
are already adding value across these stages,
starting with quoting.
We have paperless parts.
Paperless parts is a quoting and collaboration platform
that centralizes requests for quotes, pricing, and communication.
It connects design, quoting, and manufacturing processes
together, ultimately making it easier for designers
and manufacturers to collaborate across their supply chain.
Now moving into design and engineering
is a company called Szenera.
This is a no-code automation platform
for engineering workflows.
It connects design tools and lets teams
automate design logic.
So instead of repeating the same tasks,
engineers can move faster, iterate more,
and make better decisions.
Now shifting into manufacturing, Cloud NC.
Cloud NC's cam assist uses AI to generate optimized CNC
tool paths for machining.
It reduces the need for deep programming expertise
and speeds up time to production.
And then finally, supply chain.
AvNet is one of the largest global distributors
of electronic components.
Their integration brings real-time electronics component
data, availability, and pricing into the eCAD design process.
So instead of discovering issues late,
customers can make better decisions even earlier.
Now, today most customers aren't using all of these solutions
all at once.
They're starting with one, maybe two integrations.
And even that already reduces friction, improves accuracy,
and saves time.
And as they start layering in more niche solutions
that are tailored to their workflow,
the overall experience improves exponentially.
Because for customers, it's not just about any one tool.
It's about how the workflow comes together.
So if you look at that workflow, what this really enables
is more than just connecting of tools.
It allows data to move faster.
It allows decisions to get better,
and it helps errors go down.
That's what makes better outcomes for our customers.
But that flow doesn't happen on its own.
It's driven by three things.
Automation, which removes repetitive work,
connected data, so decisions are not used to it.
So decisions are made with full context
and the platform that brings systems together
so they can actually work as one.
And this is important because there are,
because these are the same three forces
that we talked about earlier.
Now you can see how they show up in real-life workflows.
And that is what's making the shift real.
So how do these integrations actually get built?
This is where the platform comes in.
Our Autodesk design and make platform
exposes a broad set of APIs across design,
manufacturing, and operations.
Everything from automating tasks to accessing models and data
to integrating with systems like ERP, PLM, and MES.
What you're seeing here is just a snapshot
of what's available today.
This is what allows partners to plug directly into workflows.
It's the technical foundation that makes these connections possible.
But APIs alone aren't enough for where things are going.
You'll be hearing a lot this week about model context protocol or MCP.
This is what will be supercharging the next wave
of manufacturing software.
APIs connect systems and MCPs enable execution.
MCPs act as an execution layer for workflows
so that AI doesn't just access data.
It can actually take action inside a real workflows.
Instead of point-to-point integrations,
you get a more dynamic context-aware set of interactions.
Tools can share data, trigger actions, and coordinate in real-time.
And this is where things connect back to a GenTik AI.
A GenTik AI doesn't just need data, it needs access,
and the ability to act.
MCP is what makes that possible.
So over time, workflows don't just become connected.
They become more intelligent and more autonomous.
And that makes one more thing extremely important,
choosing where to build.
Because as these capabilities evolve,
the value doesn't come from just any one tool on its own.
It comes from how these solutions work together.
And as more solutions connect, the value doesn't just add up.
It compounds.
And the ecosystem your solution is part of directly impacts
the value that you can deliver to your customers.
So how do you plug into the right ecosystem
and deliver that value?
Well, many of you already know the building the solution
is only part of the equation.
The hardest part is growing awareness and driving adoption.
And that's where strategic partnerships become real.
Partnerships with Autodesk help you build better,
expand your reach, and scale more effectively.
We see this as a journey.
It starts with building,
which is aligning your solution to our product roadmap
and helping integrating with the right tools.
Then comes activation.
This is where we help you take advantage of our go-to-market
engine and enable the teams working hard to grow Autodesk's
business to surface your solution alongside our own.
And ultimately, it's about scaling.
Driving adoption and growing your business through
co-marketing in-person events and other revenue generating
opportunities.
There's progression.
From building something valuable to getting it in motion,
to turning it into something that actually scales.
And here is where I invite you to consider joining
the Fusion Technology Partner Program.
Our mission is to help you move along that partner journey
as easily as possible.
From building an integration to actually building a business
around it.
Because we've seen it, just connecting to the platform
isn't enough on its own.
To support you, the program focuses on three things.
First, we provide formal technology partner status.
This isn't just a badge.
It's a trust signal backed by Autodesk.
It gives customers confidence that your solution is recognized
and adds value within the ecosystem.
Second, we provide access to Autodesk's go-to-market engine.
We make sure the right teams inside Autodesk know what you've built
and we help include your solution in our go-to-market activities.
Through campaigns, demand generation, and events
so you can expand your reach.
And lastly, we provide structured guidance to help you scale.
With us, you're not navigating Autodesk on your own.
We connect you to the right teams and help you understand
how to work with them and where your solution fits
in our platform and products.
The goal is simple, to help you move from integration
to adoption to something that actually scales.
Now, I'll leave you with this.
Rethink how you build.
Don't optimize individual steps.
Optimize the workflow.
Don't just look for features.
Look for how systems connect.
And if you're building today, especially in AEC,
consider where else your solutions could apply.
Because many of the same challenges exist in manufacturing.
And there's a real opportunity to extend what you've already built
into new workflows and adjacent markets.
And if that's something you're thinking about,
we'd love to work with you in the Fusion Technology Partner Program.
At the end of the day, this is really about delivering
better outcomes for our customers.
So what I've said today resonates with you.
I invite you to learn more about our program.
You can scan the QR code here to go visit our website.
Thank you.
